rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 4, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT DOYLE'S WORK COMPANY, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Grant. No opposition filed. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT D. CAIRO VS. SAFEWAY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery, Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 4, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT SAFEWAY, INC.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery, Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Off Calendar. Notice of settlement filed by Plaintiff on September 20, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HAIQING QIU VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition; Motion To Compel Production Of Documents; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 4, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT DIGNITY COMMUNITY CARE DBA SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL's Motion To Compel Deposition; Motion To Compel Production Of Documents; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiff HAIQING QIU to appear for deposition and bring the requested documents on a mutually agreed upon date no later than November 4, 2019 at 10:00 a.m. at SHEUERMAN, MARTINI, TABARI, ZENERE & GARVIN 1033 Willow Street, San Jose, CA 95125. If the parties cannot agree on a mutually agreed upon date, then the deposition will be held on November 4, 2019 at 10:00 a.m. Request for sanctions are granted in the amount of $1835.05 against plaintiff HAIQING QIU and are to be paid no later than November 4, 2019. Payment to made payable to "SHEUERMAN, MARTINI, TABARI, ZENERE & GARVIN Client Trust Account". Moving party to submit proposed order to the court at time of hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER COOPER VS. EMERALD FUND, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joint Motion And Motion By Defendants Emeral D100 Van Ness Llc And Emerald Fund, Inc. To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 4, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANT EMERALD FUND, INC., EMERALD 100 VAN NESS LLC's, Joint Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint. CCP §435.5 provides: "Before filing a motion to strike…the moving party shall meet and confer in person or by telephone." Counsel's declaration indicates this has not occurred, so the motion is taken off calendar. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately. Defendant has until November 4, 2019 to file a responsive pleading to the complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(RBU/302)
